research_runs.focus: Public interviews, direct quotes, project-page statements, and social quotes
Public X launch posts and follow-up corpus
First-party project pages and methodology text
Idea generation, project selection, scoping, and shipping model
Thinking style, tacit knowledge, attention habits, and cognitive pattern
Public code, libraries, architecture, scraping, storage, deployment, and trade-offs
Taste, aesthetics, likes, dislikes, labels, UI, and humor
Launch timelines, press pickup, reactions, shutdowns, archiving, and iteration
Lesser-known projects, collaborators, events, and experiments
Ethics, public data boundaries, shutdown triggers, safety model, and risk controls
Structured non-impersonating AI context system synthesis
Expanded source map, reliability tiers, first-party pages, press, and source inventory

source_reliability_tiers.name: Primary and first-party
source_reliability_tiers.use: Highest confidence for project descriptions, methodology, public self-description, and interface text

source_reliability_tiers.name: Major press and interview coverage
source_reliability_tiers.use: High confidence for direct quotes, dates, public reactions, and third-party context

source_reliability_tiers.name: Secondary mentions and social chatter
source_reliability_tiers.use: Discovery and public-reaction context only; cross-check before factual use

claim_categories.claim: Riley Walz's public project process emphasizes rapid weekend-scale execution.
A recurring idea source is overlooked public data residue or platform quirks.
He publicly prefers simple engineering labels over grand artistic labels.
His taste repeatedly includes nostalgia, old-internet artifacts, urban systems, and playful surveillance inversion.
Some public projects operate in ethical gray zones and should be adapted with stricter safety controls.

known_limitations: The research used available public web sources and public social references. A dedicated xAI Search API was not exposed as a callable tool in this environment.
Some X links and quoted posts are available through embeds, press citations, or public URLs but may not be fully accessible depending on platform restrictions.
Press articles can paraphrase or excerpt interviews; quote confidence is strongest when triangulated with first-party pages or multiple reputable outlets.
The model should not infer private psychology from public artifacts.
